headlines.title: Asian shares mostly slip as latest fighting undermines the US-Iran ceasefire.
'Punch' the viral monkey at Japan zoo draws 43 mil. yen in donations as deadline extended
Japan one step from historic yen collapse, SMBC Nikko warns
Katayama restates readiness to act on yen after data shows record intervention
Japan's Nikkei retreats from record peak as traders gauge fragile Mideast peace talks
Japan's Nikkei retreats from record peak as traders gauge fragile Mideast peace talks
Japan's Nikkei retreats from record peak as traders gauge fragile Mideast peace talks

marketTone.ko: 오늘 일본 증시는 닛케이 지수가 사상 최고치에서 후퇴하는 등 아시아 증시 전반의 하락세를 보였습니다. 이는 중동 지역의 지정학적 긴장 고조와 불안정한 평화 회담에 대한 투자자들의 경계심이 주된 요인으로 작용했습니다. 한편, 엔화는 SMBC 닛코가 '역사적 붕괴' 가능성을 경고할 정도로 심각한 약세를 보였으며, 이에 카타야마 등 당국자들은 최근 기록적인 개입에 이어 추가 개입 준비 태세를 재확인했습니다.
marketTone.en: Japanese stocks, represented by the Nikkei, retreated from record peaks today, mirroring a broader slip in Asian shares. This downturn is primarily driven by renewed geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, as traders gauge the fragility of peace talks and reports of fighting undermine a US-Iran ceasefire. Concurrently, the yen faces significant pressure, with SMBC Nikko warning of a potential "historic collapse," prompting officials like Katayama to reiterate readiness for intervention following recent record actions.
